seoTitle: Pampoolah vs Pan Ban — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Pampoolah and Pan Ban. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Pampoolah vs Pan Ban: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pampoolah and Pan Ban.
heading: Pampoolah vs Pan Ban — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pampoolah, New South Wales and Pan Ban, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pampoolah has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Pan Ban (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pampoolah or Pan Ban safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pampoolah has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Pan Ban scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pampoolah scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Pan Ban scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pampoolah or Pan Ban?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pampoolah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.3% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
